title: NOC 31102 – General Practitioners & Family Physicians | GTR Immigration
description: Family doctors are the backbone of primary healthcare in Canada. Due to physician shortages, aging demographics, and increased healthcare demand, General Practitioners (GPs) and Family Physicians remain one of the most actively recruited professions nationwide. Classified under NOC 31102 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: General Practitioners and Family Physicians typically:
Examine patients and review medical histories
Order laboratory tests, X-rays, and diagnostics
Diagnose and treat illnesses and injuries
Deliver babies and provide prenatal/postnatal care
Provide health counseling and disease prevention advice
Coordinate long-term and continuous patient care
Report births, deaths, and communicable diseases to authorities
employment_requirements: Graduation from an approved medical school
Completion of qualifying exams through the Medical Council of Canada or Collège des médecins du Québec (for Quebec)
Without provincial licensing, physicians cannot independently practice in Canada.
job_outlook: This indicates a significant shortage, particularly in:
Family Physicians remain one of the strongest healthcare immigration pathways in Canada.
🩺 Planning Canada PR as a Family Physician?
GTR Immigration can evaluate your profile under NOC 31102, guide you through MCC exams and provincial licensing, and structure a strategic pathway to practice and permanent residence in Canada.
pathways: One of Canada’s highest projected healthcare shortages
High earning potential under fee-for-service or blended payment models
Opportunities to establish private or group practice
additional_information: Family Physicians are urgently needed in:
Community health clinicsFamily practice clinicsHospitals & urgent care centersLong-term care facilitiesRural and underserved communities
Typical annual earnings: $76,800–$452,000 CAD, depending on billing model, province, patient load, and practice type.
Many provinces offer signing bonuses, relocation incentives, and rural practice grants.
